concepts.title: Condition for line to be Tangent and Secant
concepts.conceptBody:
Intersection of a line and parabola

For a parabola ${{\text{y}}^{2}}=4\text{ax}$ and a line $\text{y}=\text{mx}+\text{c}$. there are 3 cases possible :

  1. The line and the parabola do not intersect.
  2. The line and the parabola intersect at one point only. This is the case of touching the parabola i.e. tangent to parabola.
  3. The line and parabola intersect at 2 points i.e., the line has a line segment which acts as chord to parabola.

The above 3 conditions are algebraically equivalent to saying that the quadratic equation formed by the intersection of two curves will either have:

  1. No roots.
  2. Only one real root.
  3. 2 distinct real roots.

So putting value of $y$, i.e. $y=mx+c$ in ${{y}^{2}}=4ax$

 $\begin{array}{l} {{(\text{mx}+\text{c})}^{2}} =4\text{ax} \\ \Rightarrow {{\text{m}}^{2}}{{\text{x}}^{2}}+{{\text{c}}^{2}}+2\text{mcx} =4\text{ax} \\ \Rightarrow {{\text{m}}^{2}}{{\text{x}}^{2}}+\left( 2\text{mc}-4\text{a} \right)\text{x}+{{\text{c}}^{2}} =0 \\ \end{array}$

Discriminant of this equation

$\begin{array}{l} {{D}^{2}} ~={{(2mc-4a)}^{2}}-4{{m}^{2}}{{c}^{2}} \\ ~=4\left\{ {{(mc-2a)}^{2}}-{{m}^{2}}{{c}^{2}} \right\} \\ ~=16\left( {{a}^{2}}-amc \right) \\ \end{array}$

Now for our 3 cases

  1. No real roots, $\text{D}<0\Rightarrow \text{a}<\text{mc}$ i.e. no intersection.
  2. Only one real root, $\text{D}=0\Rightarrow \text{a}=\text{mc}$ i.e., tangent.
  3. 2 real distinct roots $\text{D}>0\Rightarrow \text{a}>\text{mc}$ i.e., chord to parabola.

Let us discuss the case 3 first and find out the length of chord formed.

Case 3 : Let the line meet parabola in 2 distinct points $\left( {{x}_{1}},{{y}_{1}} \right)$ and $\left( {{x}_{2}},{{y}_{2}} \right)$. Then the

length of chord will be :

$\sqrt{{{\left( {{\text{x}}_{2}}-{{\text{x}}_{1}} \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( {{\text{y}}_{2}}-{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)}^{2}}}=\sqrt{{{\left( {{\text{x}}_{2}}-{{\text{x}}_{1}} \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( \text{m}{{\text{x}}_{2}}+\text{c}-\text{m}{{\text{x}}_{1}}-\text{c} \right)}^{2}}}$

$\begin{array}{l} ~=\sqrt{{{\left( {{x}_{2}}-{{x}_{1}} \right)}^{2}}+{{m}^{2}}{{\left( {{x}_{2}}-{{x}_{1}} \right)}^{2}}} \\ ~=\sqrt{1+{{m}^{2}}}\sqrt{{{\left( {{x}_{2}}-{{x}_{1}} \right)}^{2}}} \\ ~=\sqrt{1+{{m}^{2}}}\sqrt{{{\left( {{x}_{1}}+{{x}_{2}} \right)}^{2}}-4{{x}_{1}}{{x}_{2}}} \\ ~=\sqrt{1+{{m}^{2}}}\sqrt{\frac{4{{(mc-2a)}^{2}}}{{{m}^{4}}}-\frac{4{{c}^{2}}}{{{m}^{2}}}} \\ \end{array}$

because from (i) ${{x}_{1}}+{{x}_{2}}=\frac{-2\left( mc-2a \right)}{{{m}^{2}}}$

$\begin{array}{l} {{x}_{1}}{{x}_{2}} ~=\frac{{{c}^{2}}}{{{m}^{2}}} \\ ~=\frac{2}{{{m}^{2}}}\sqrt{1+{{m}^{2}}}\sqrt{{{(mc-2a)}^{2}}-{{c}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}} \\ ~=\frac{2}{{{m}^{2}}}\sqrt{1+{{m}^{2}}}\sqrt{4{{a}^{2}}-4amc} \\ ~=\frac{4}{{{m}^{2}}}\sqrt{1+{{m}^{2}}}\sqrt{{{a}^{2}}-amc} \\ ~=\frac{4}{{{m}^{2}}}\sqrt{1+{{m}^{2}}}\sqrt{a\left( a-mc \right)} \\ \end{array}$

So the length of chord cut by the line $=\frac{4}{{{m}^{2}}}\sqrt{1+{{m}^{2}}}\sqrt{a\left( a-mc \right)}$ 

Now let us discuss the case 2 .

Case 2 : Condition for tangency : 

as done earlier, for this $D=0$ then the condition is $~'c'$ $=\frac{\text{a}}{\text{m}}$

So putting this value of $\text{c}$ in line to get the tangent equation

$y=mx+\frac{a}{m}$

Point of contact : 

Putting $\text{c}=\frac{\text{a}}{\text{m}}$ in quadratic equation for finding the points on parabola.

${{\text{m}}^{2}}{{\text{x}}^{2}}+\left( 2\text{ m}\left( \frac{\text{a}}{\text{m}} \right)-4\text{a} \right)\text{x}+\frac{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}{{{\text{m}}^{2}}}=0$

$\begin{array}{l} {{\text{m}}^{2}}{{\text{x}}^{2}}-2\text{ax}+\frac{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}{{{\text{m}}^{2}}} =0 \\ {{\left( \text{mx}-\frac{\text{a}}{\text{m}} \right)}^{2}} =0 \\ \Rightarrow \text{ x} =\frac{\text{a}}{{{\text{m}}^{2}}} \\ \end{array}$

Putting this equation of line to get value of $y$

$y=mx+\frac{a}{m}=m\left( \frac{a}{{{m}^{2}}} \right)+\frac{a}{m}=\frac{2a}{m}$

$\therefore $ Point of contact becomes $:\left( \frac{\text{a}}{{{\text{m}}^{2}}},\frac{2\mathbf{a}}{\text{m}} \right)$
